definition:  Shovelware refers to low-quality software, games, or digital content produced cheaply and quickly, often in large volumes, focusing on quantity rather than quality. The term originates from the idea of “shoveling” useless data onto CDs or digital stores, commonly featuring poor performance, bugs, and little value
which we do enjoy our bad games
but flash games is an example that will be forgotten

but ya you could consider many nes games as shovelware,  but for the most part i felt like there was still passion, which ya in sure many are passionate about it.  but ya some flash games are probably forever forgotten

ware that is available free of charge and often distributed informally for evaluation, after which a fee may be requested for continued use.  kinda re-educating myself as i was quite sure this is what this was
